title: Bridge Paper: Panpsychism and dualism in the science of consciousness.
author: Frontier Science / Hermes T2

body.text: This bridge paper connects the esoteric/philosophical concept of **Dependent Arising** with mechanistic science from PubMed/MEDLINE.
**Bridge thesis:** Predictive processing — free energy principle, Bayesian brain, active inference, neural causality.
**Phase context:** predictive coding, Bayesian brain, free energy, active inference, predictive processing

Paper: Panpsychism and dualism in the science of consciousness.
DOI: 10.1016/j.neubiorev.2024.105845
Abstract: A resurgence of panpsychism and dualism is a matter of ongoing debate in modern neuroscience. Although metaphysically hostile, panpsychism and dualism both persist in the science of consciousness because the former is proposed as a straightforward answer to the problem of integrating consciousness into the fabric of physical reality, whereas the latter proposes a simple solution to the problem of free will by endowing consciousness with causal power as a prerequisite for moral responsibility.
